title: Peppery Chewy Slovakian Tamarind Dumplings
description: A quick, low‑carb Slovakian twist on dumplings, featuring a tangy tamarind core and a spicy pepper kick.
instructions: In a medium bowl whisk together almond flour, coconut flour, xanthan gum, salt, black pepper, smoked paprika, and crushed red pepper flakes until evenly mixed.
In a separate bowl beat the egg, then stir in the tamarind pulp and olive oil until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients, stirring until a firm dough forms; if the dough feels too dry, add 1 Tbsp water, if too sticky, add a little more almond flour.
Let the dough rest for 5 minutes to allow the flours to hydrate.
Divide the dough into 12 equal portions and roll each into a smooth ball.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil; add the dumplings in batches, cooking 4–5 minutes or until they float and feel tender.
Remove dumplings with a slotted spoon and transfer to a skillet over medium heat; drizzle the remaining 1 Tbsp olive oil and sauté for 1 minute to crisp the edges.
Plate the dumplings hot, sprinkle with chopped cilantro if desired, and serve immediately.
